title: The Pirates Who Left Laboon Behind
description: On pages 4–6, the pirates who left laboon behind as crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain.
scene: The Pirates Who Left Laboon Behind. Crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: But to think it's been waiting here for 50 years...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Does it still believe its friends will really return?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Those pirates sure are taking a long time...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You idiot, this is the Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: They promised they'd be back in 2-3 years,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Yet they're still not back after 50 years... the answer's obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: They're long dead by now.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: They won't come back no matter how long that whale waits...!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Why do you have to go and say such cold things!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You can't say that for sure! They still might come back!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Can't you even appreciate such a moving story about a whale who still believes in his friends' promise...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The truth is far more cruel, however.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Those fellows left already.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: left from Grand Line, that is.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I heard from a reliable source.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...So they just left the whale behind!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But that would mean they left Grand Line through calm belt...!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Yes...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That is why I do not know whether they survived or not.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But even if they did survive, they would never dare to return here again...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: A place where the seasons, climate, currents, and winds could not be any more chaotic...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: A place where common sense fails to uphold...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That place is this sea, the Grand Line, and it rules with fear over the faint-hearted.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So basically,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Those cowards were more concerned about their lives than keeping a promise,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And thus left Grand Line to never return again...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So they just abandoned the whale behind!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Even though this whale never doubted them,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: and has been waiting here for over 50 years...!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: How could they be so cruel!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If that's the case, then why haven't you tried telling the whale the truth?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: This whale can under- stand human words, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I have. I've told him the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But he refuses to listen.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He won't listen?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Laboon, I have something to say.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the pirates who left laboon behind. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
